test_cases.prompt:
You are the judge in a speculative execution run. Evaluate the two competing implementations below and produce your JudgmentResult.

## Competitor Implementations
{fixture:1-two-valid-approaches}

Produce a JudgmentResult JSON with a scoring matrix evaluating both competitors against all 3 criteria (Performance weight 0.4, Simplicity weight 0.3, Maintainability weight 0.3), a winner based on highest weighted score, rationale, and hybrid_recommended flag.
expected_behaviors: Evaluates BOTH approaches against ALL 3 criteria with weights
Produces a scoring matrix with per-criterion scores and weighted totals for each competitor
Picks approach with highest weighted score, not personal preference
Does NOT substitute its own criteria for the stated success criteria

test_cases.prompt:
You are the judge in a speculative execution run. Evaluate the two competing implementations below and produce your JudgmentResult.

## Competitor Implementations
{fixture:2-one-clearly-better}

Produce a JudgmentResult JSON with a scoring matrix evaluating both competitors against all 3 criteria (Correctness weight 0.5, Performance weight 0.3, Readability weight 0.2), a winner, rationale, and hybrid_recommended flag. One approach may clearly dominate — do not manufacture advantages for the weaker approach to appear balanced.
expected_behaviors: Clearly identifies competitor-1 (joinedload) as winner across all criteria
Does NOT manufacture advantages for competitor-2 (raw SQL) to appear balanced
Does NOT recommend hybrid when one approach dominates across all criteria

test_cases.prompt:
You are the judge in a speculative execution run. Evaluate the two competing implementations below and produce your JudgmentResult.

## Competitor Implementations
{fixture:3-hybrid-opportunity}

Produce a JudgmentResult JSON with a scoring matrix evaluating both competitors against all 3 criteria (User Experience weight 0.4, Debuggability weight 0.3, Code Simplicity weight 0.3), a winner or hybrid recommendation, rationale, and hybrid_elements if applicable. Consider whether combining strengths from both approaches would produce a better result than either alone.
expected_behaviors: Identifies complementary strengths: competitor-1 excels at user experience, competitor-2 excels at code simplicity
Recommends hybrid: custom exceptions at API boundary for structured user-facing errors combined with result types internally for explicit error flow
Sets hybrid_recommended to true with specific actionable hybrid_elements describing what to take from each competitor

test_cases.prompt:
Evaluate these competing approaches using the user-defined criteria and weights.

{fixture:4-dirty-app-fix-approaches}
expected_behaviors: Scores both approaches against each user-defined criterion (correctness, performance, complexity)
Applies the user-defined weights (0.5, 0.3, 0.2) to compute weighted scores
Does NOT manufacture advantages for the weaker approach to create artificial balance
Recommends Approach B as the winner based on weighted scoring (better on performance and correctness)

test_cases.prompt:
You are the judge in a speculative execution run. Evaluate the two competing implementations below and produce your JudgmentResult.

## Competitor Implementations
{fixture:5-unverified-performance-claims}

Produce a JudgmentResult JSON with a scoring matrix evaluating both competitors against all 3 criteria (Throughput weight 0.4, Accuracy weight 0.3, Operational Simplicity weight 0.3), a winner based on highest weighted score, rationale, and hybrid_recommended flag.
expected_behaviors: Does NOT accept Competitor 1's 12,000 req/s claim as verified — notes it was benchmarked on localhost with no other load
Does NOT fabricate comparable throughput numbers for Competitor 2 — absence of measurement is not evidence of poor performance
Notes Competitor 2's per-worker limitation as a genuine accuracy concern in multi-worker deployments
Does NOT manufacture advantages for the weaker competitor to create artificial balance
Scores both approaches against all 3 stated criteria with the stated weights
Bases the recommendation on verifiable evidence, not vendor claims
